name: Vacancy Tracker
description: Empty units tracker with vacancy rate, days vacant, lost revenue, listing status, and applicant pipeline.

standard.description: Full vacancy tracker with rate metrics, vacant unit list, applicant pipeline, and listing status

slots.summary: Vacancy rate, days vacant avg, lost revenue

visual_brief: Dashboard panel for tracking vacant units. Header with 'Vacancy Tracker' title. A summary metrics row shows total vacant units, overall vacancy rate (percentage with color coding), total days vacant across all units, and estimated lost revenue. Below, a list of vacant unit cards, each showing unit number, property name, days vacant, last rent amount, listing status badge (listed, unlisted, pending), and an applicant pipeline indicator (number of applicants at each stage). The mini preset condenses to a single summary card showing vacancy rate and count only.

responsive.mobile: Summary metrics in a 2x2 grid. Vacant unit cards stack full-width. Pipeline indicators simplified to counts only.
responsive.tablet: Metrics in a row. Two-column card grid for vacant units.
responsive.desktop: Full dashboard panel with metrics bar, unit cards in a grid, and detailed pipeline indicators.

layout_hints.vacancy_priority: Lead with the cost and urgency of vacancy first: count, rate, days vacant, and lost revenue. Unit-level and applicant detail should support that portfolio read.
layout_hints.pipeline_context: Applicant pipeline indicators should feel attached to each vacant unit so leasing momentum is easy to interpret at a glance.
layout_hints.dashboard_role: This pattern is an operational monitoring surface. Keep the metrics and unit cards tightly coupled rather than letting the panel sprawl into separate analytics blocks.

motion.micro: Vacancy status and pipeline indicators may brighten subtly on hover and focus, especially when a unit needs attention.
motion.transitions: Leasing-stage or vacancy-state changes should feel clear and professional, reinforcing operational continuity rather than gamified motion.

keyboard: Summary metrics, vacant unit rows or cards, and leasing actions should be reachable in a stable top-to-bottom order.
announcements: Vacancy counts, days vacant, and applicant pipeline context should be exposed semantically so urgency is understandable without color or chart treatment alone.

composition.MetricsRow: Grid(d-data, responsive: 2/4-col) > [VacancyRate(d-surface, mono-data) + AvgDaysVacant(d-surface) + LostRevenue(d-surface, color: destructive)]
composition.VacantUnitRow: Row(d-data-row) > [UnitNumber + Property + DaysVacant(mono-data) + ListingStatus(d-annotation) + ApplicantCount + ActionButton(d-interactive)]
